rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Statement Of Interest And Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate And Authorizing Distribution Directly To Celtic Research

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2024. 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The address of the attorney for Bridge Financial Funding listed on the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form on file is incorrect. 4) The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be specified. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 5) Please provide a copy of notice of proposed action given before donating the estate's tangible persona property. See Probate Code section 10513. The information required by CRC 7.250(a) must also be provided. 6) The schedule of disbursements does not show payment of the probate referee's commission or court filing fees, and no reimbursement of such costs has been sought. Who has advanced those undisclosed expenses of administration? 7) No bank statement has been filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. 8) The estate cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. Please provide a proof that the cash is now fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). 9) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee and assignee. The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 10) The first account period cannot exceed 2 years from the date of death. No prior court approval has been obtained to enlarge the account period. LRSF 14.34(A). 11) Is the estate ready to be closed, even though the administrator has not recovered proceeds of Macy shares from the Controller?

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALETTA MARIE LURIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amendment was not e-filed until 2/14/24. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring the notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (The proof of service shows that notice was mailed on 2/12/24, even before the amendment was uploaded for e-filing.) (2) Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The calculation of the proposed distribution shown in paragraph 32, subparagraph C, did not reflect (subtract) the $500 reserve. A second amendment to the amended petition must be filed to show the correct calculation of the proposed distribution. Notice of the second amendment must be served after it is e-filed. Please provide a courtesy copy of the second amendment to the amended petition and the proof of service of notice of the amendment and the second amendment to the assigned examiner by 3/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (JC form DE-300 is meant to be attached to the original 13100 affidavit. Its separate filing in this instance will be accepted, but please make a note of it for future.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now, a "revised" petition has been filed. (2) No proof of service of the revised petition has been filed. (3) Proof of publication of notice of the revised petition must be filed. (4) The caption of the revised petition is incomplete. (5) No will has been lodged yet and a copy of the will is not attached to the revised petition as Attachment 3f(2). (6) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number filed on 1/17/24 shows the name as Shanda Williams. (7) The revised petition still states that the decedent died on 5/24/21, but the proposed order submitted to the Court shows the date of death as 6/4/20. (8) Item 3g(2) of the petition should NOT be completed if there is a will. (9) Item 5a(2) of the revised petition is still incomplete, and item 5a(3) or (4) must be answered. (10) Are Shawny Williams and Shambric Williams adults? The actual ages of adults do not need to be set forth in item 8 to a petition for probate; just "adult" is sufficient. (11) How is daughter-in-law Naela Calderon an interested party? Is she named in the will that has not yet been lodged with the Court or attached to any petition? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Petitioner is strongly urged to hire an attorney if she intends to proceed and has standing. If these issues are not sufficiently addressed by the 4/10/24 hearing, an appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if the revised petition should be taken off calendar.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BELEN PATRICIA BARRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A person seeking appointment as a fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] Neither the "declaration" filed on 12/8/23 nor the "supplemental declaration" filed on 2/6/24 appears to have been signed and verified by the petitioner. (2) Form DE-147S did not show the name as "Patricia S. Barrera; the name on the form filed on 12/15/23 is Belen S. Barrer. Deadline is 3/29/24 for providing file stamped courtesy copies of the petitioner's supplement to her petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Off calendar

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SIMON JEFFERSON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION For: (1) Financial Elder Abuse Under Welf. & Inst. Code Secs 15610.310 And 15657.3; (2) Conversion; (3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (4) Adjudication Of Rights As To Personal Property Under Probate Code Sec 850, Et Seq.,Including An Award Of Double Damages And Attorney'S Fees Under Probate Code Sec 859; (5) Constructive Trust Under Civ. Code Sec 2223; And (6) Accounting.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/10/2024, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) Respondent must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. The proof of service attached to the second supplement indicates that the respondent was served by substituted service on 12/19/2023, less than 40 days before the 01/22/2024 hearing. See CCP section 415.20(a). Also, the process server's declaration of mailing required by that section was not attached to the proof of service. Note: If the respondent's attorney has authority to accept service, the mandatory Judicial Council form POS-015 must be used. The date of acknowledgment and receipt by the respondent's attorney must be at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) The first supplement did not list all the beneficiaries named in the trust (Patricia Moore, Romey Jefferson, Gail Thornton (or Gail Thorman). If any beneficiary is deceased, his/her date of death must be stated. Please also identify all persons who would be the decedent's heirs if the decedent had died intestate and provide a family tree including the names and dates of death of all predeceased heirs (parents, siblings, nieces, nephews). All heirs and devisees must be identified and receive notice to the extent the matter concerns the decedent's estate. Section 851(b)(1). All beneficiaries of the trust must be identified and receive notice to the extent the matter concerns the decedent's trust and any relief asserted against the trustee. Section 851(b)(3).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FLOERSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed. (5) The petition caption is missing the full address of the petitioner. (6) The petitioner is purported to be Danielle Floersch (item 2 of the petition), but David Floersch is also a petitioner? (7) The Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities shows the address of David Floersch, not the Truckee P.O. Box claimed to be Petitioner's address in item 8 of the petition. (8) Item 7a or b of the petition must be checked. (9) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When did the decedent's parents die? Whose children are the nephews? (10) If the whereabouts of a person entitled to notice are unknown, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice to that person be dispensed with. There must be an adequate showing of the diligent search for such person. [CRC 7.52.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILMA V. GARDNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. (The name of the proposed personal representative was missing in item 4 of the waiver of bond, but, in any event, it is presumed that a petitioner would waive bond for himself.) Status of closing the estate is set for 3/10/2025. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 3/10/2025 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHEE WANG CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2024. Notes emailed to petitioner on 02/28/2024.
